interactive_prompts.name: trust_prompt
interactive_prompts.regex: Do you trust the contents of this directory|Trust this directory|Working with untrusted contents|This folder has not been trusted|Yes, continue
interactive_prompts.note: codex 0.134+ asks to trust dir on first launch; '1' = yes/trust. Regex broadened per cycle-121 research dossier (codex-cli-0.134-repl-boot-timeout-2026-05-28.md) to also catch the 'Working with untrusted contents' / 'Yes, continue' phrasings emitted on worktree paths not pre-trusted in ~/.codex/config.toml.

interactive_prompts.name: per_edit_approval
interactive_prompts.regex: Would you like to make the following edits|Press enter to confirm or esc to cancel|Yes, proceed
interactive_prompts.note: Cycle-124 G1b (defense-in-depth for G1a's `--yolo`): codex per-edit-approval modal that hung cycle-123 tdd. The modal lists 3 numbered options ('1. Yes, proceed' / '2. Yes, and don't ask again' / '3. No, and tell Codex what to do differently'); response_keys '1,Enter' selects 'Yes, proceed' explicitly (the default highlight is option 1 so a bare 'Enter' would also work, but the explicit '1' is robust to future UX shifts in default selection). With G1a's `--yolo` in default_args, this modal should not fire — it's the regex backstop for the case where `--yolo` regresses (codex renames the flag, operator overrides default_args, OR the modal appears via a different code path codex's approval=never doesn't cover). Source: docs/incidents/cycle-123-codex-edit-approval-modal-and-empty-fallback-chain.md.

interactive_prompts.name: auth_recheck
interactive_prompts.regex: Please log in|sign in to ChatGPT|Authentication required

note: Interactive codex via tmux. Preserves ChatGPT-account subscription billing (vs codex exec, which may meter differently). Same model_tier_map as the codex-headless driver. chatgpt_safe_models/chatgpt_default_model (cycle-142): on a ChatGPT/subscription account the backend 400-rejects gpt-5.4/gpt-5.5 (API-key-only by plan tier — see OpenAI codex issues #14190/#19654), so the driver clamps balanced/deep tiers down to gpt-5.4-mini when auth=chatgpt. API-key auth keeps the larger models.
